reactions: An eyelid twitch is the smallest rebellion a body can stage. And the most irritating.

entries.intro: I walked barefoot on warm stone and it was the best decision I've made all week.
details: The heat came up through the soles and into my whole body. Smooth, solid warmth.
I stood there with my eyes closed, feeling the ground like it was new.
reactions: Warm stone underfoot is a pleasure that costs nothing and delivers everything.

entries.intro: I dreamed I was late for something. Woke up with my heart racing and no idea what I'd missed.
details: The urgency was real but the event was imaginary. Classic. The body panicked for nothing.
I lay in bed with leftover dread, waiting for it to drain. It took longer than it should.
reactions: Dreaming you're late is the mind rehearsing a disaster it doesn't need to prepare for.

statCheck.pass: I've been feeling behind on everything lately. Not just in dreams: in life. The dream is honest about what I won't say during the day.
statCheck.fail: Just a bad dream. Took a minute to shake off.

entries.intro: I noticed a flower growing through a crack in the stone. Tiny and stubborn.
details: No soil to speak of, no shelter from the wind. Just a crack and the will to fill it.
It was small and pale and utterly determined. I admired it more than I should admit.
reactions: A flower in a crack is proof that persistence doesn't need permission.

entries.intro: I've been thirsty all day no matter how much I drink. The water just vanishes.
details: Cup after cup and the thirst comes back within minutes. I'm a bottomless well running in reverse.
I don't know if it's the heat or the dryness or just my body being difficult.
reactions: Unquenchable thirst is the body filing a complaint you can't resolve fast enough.

statCheck.pass: I've been eating poorly and sleeping worse. The thirst is probably just the body's way of telling me something's off that water alone won't fix.
statCheck.fail: Just a dry day. The heat does that.

entries.intro: The moon was full last night and bright enough to read by. I know because I tried.
details: Silver light through the window, casting sharp shadows across the floor.
I held a page up to the moonlight and could almost make out the words. Almost.
reactions: A full moon is beautiful but a poor substitute for a candle.

entries.intro: I ate too much at dinner and spent the evening regretting it. Couldn't move, couldn't think.
details: The food was good, so I had more. Then more. The plate was emptied by enthusiasm, not hunger.
I sat in the chair like a sack of grain, breathing carefully.
reactions: Overeating is the only crime where the punishment starts immediately.

statCheck.pass: I eat too much when I'm trying to fill something that isn't hunger. The plate runs out but the emptiness doesn't.
statCheck.fail: Just got carried away. The food was good.

entries.intro: The first real day of warmth and I left the door open all afternoon. Fresh air running through the house.
details: The breeze carried out the stale winter air and replaced it with something green and alive.
I could hear the outside world from every room. The house felt connected to the day.
reactions: An open door on a warm day turns a house from a shelter into an invitation.

entries.intro: I twisted my ankle on uneven ground. Nothing broken, just swollen and grumpy.
details: It puffed up by evening and turned a color that ankles shouldn't be.
I've been hobbling with exaggerated care. Every step is a negotiation.
reactions: A twisted ankle is the ground's way of reminding you to look where you walk.

entries.intro: I watched the fire for an hour last night. Not for warmth: just the movement.
details: The flames never make the same shape twice. You can stare and stare and it never repeats.
I didn't think about anything in particular. The fire did the thinking for me.
reactions: Watching fire is the oldest entertainment. It's held up better than most.

statCheck.pass: I do this more often now: sit alone, watch the fire, let the quiet settle. It's not loneliness, it's a need for stillness that I didn't have when I was younger.
statCheck.fail: Just zoned out. The fire's hypnotic like that.

entries.intro: I got caught in the rain without a cloak and had to run. Undignified but effective.
details: Arms pumping, boots splashing, rain in my eyes. The last twenty paces were a sprint.
I arrived at the door gasping and dripping. The rain applauded my retreat.
reactions: Running in rain is the adult version of admitting you didn't plan ahead.

entries.intro: I heard an owl last night. Close, loud, and deeply uninterested in my sleep.
details: A low, repeating call right outside the window. Patient and relentless.
I put the pillow over my head and the owl adjusted its volume. Or my attention did.
reactions: An owl outside your window is a neighbor who keeps different hours and doesn't care.

statCheck.pass: I was already awake when the owl started. The call didn't wake me: it just filled the silence I was already sitting in.
statCheck.fail: Just a noisy bird. Kept me up for a bit.

entries.intro: I had {food_type} for breakfast and it set me up for the whole morning. Just the right start.
details: Warm, solid, filling. The kind of meal that makes you feel ready for whatever comes.
I didn't need to eat again until well past midday. The morning carried itself.
reactions: A good breakfast is the foundation. Everything that follows is built on it.

entries.intro: I got dust in both eyes at the same time. Instant blindness and regret.
details: A gust of wind carrying half the road directly into my face.
I stood there blinking and tearing up, rubbing my eyes like a child. The wind had moved on.
reactions: Dust in the eyes is the wind's cheapest trick and it works every time.

entries.intro: I woke up in the middle of the night absolutely certain I'd left something open. I hadn't.
details: I got up, checked every door and window, found everything secure, and went back to bed.
The certainty was so strong I checked again an hour later. Still closed. The mind invents problems at night.
reactions: A nighttime worry about something you already handled is the brain charging overtime for nothing.

statCheck.pass: I check things more than I used to: doors, latches, the fire, and the checking is getting worse. I know it's not rational but knowing doesn't stop it.
statCheck.fail: Just one of those half-awake worries. Gone by morning.

entries.intro: I sat in the shade on a hot day and the temperature difference was startling. Two steps between worlds.
details: Sun to shade: a ten-degree drop. The cool air felt like stepping into water.
I stayed put. The sun could wait. The shade had everything I needed.
reactions: Shade on a hot day is the most valuable real estate you don't have to pay for.

entries.intro: I sneezed so hard I pulled something in my side. A sneeze shouldn't cause injury.
details: One explosive burst and then a stabbing ache under my ribs. The sneeze left a souvenir.
I'm breathing carefully now. Deep breaths aggravate it. Shallow ones feel insufficient.
reactions: Injuring yourself with a sneeze is the body's cruelest practical joke.

entries.intro: I noticed the light changing earlier each evening. The season's turning and it shows in the sky.
details: The gold comes sooner now, that low, warm light that makes everything look softer.
A week ago dusk was an hour later. The shift is subtle until you notice it, then it's obvious.
reactions: The changing light is the truest calendar. No numbers, no names: just longer or shorter.

statCheck.pass: I watch for it every year. The exact evening when the light tips. Most people don't notice until the cold comes, but I know it by the angle of the shadows weeks before.
statCheck.fail: Just the seasons doing what they do. Happens every year.
